QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

function.arguments:
{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
assign_counts AS (
  SELECT case_id, count(*) AS cnt, min(owner_id) AS owner_id
  FROM owner_assigns
  GROUP BY case_id
),
single_assign_cases AS (
  SELECT case_id, owner_id FROM assign_counts WHERE cnt = 1
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
)
SELECT sa.owner_id AS agent_id,
       count(c.id) AS cases_handled,
       avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (c.closeddate - c.createddate))) AS avg_handle_seconds
FROM single_assign_cases sa
JOIN cases_in_period c ON regexp_replace(trim(c.id),'^#','') = sa.case_id
GROUP BY sa.owner_id
HAVING count(c.id) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
),
case_assign_counts AS (
  SELECT ca.case_id, count(*) AS assign_count,
         min(ca.owner_id) FILTER (WHERE ca.owner_id IS NOT NULL) AS sole_owner
  FROM owner_assigns ca
  WHERE ca.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY ca.case_id
),
agent_involvements AS (
  SELECT oa.owner_id AS agent_id, count(DISTINCT oa.case_id) AS cases_involved
  FROM owner_assigns oa
  WHERE oa.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY oa.owner_id
),
single_assign_case_times AS (
  SELECT cac.sole_owner AS agent_id,
         (c.closeddate - c.createddate) AS duration
  FROM case_assign_counts cac
  JOIN cases_in_period c ON c.id IS NOT NULL AND regexp_replace(trim(c.id),'^#','') = cac.case_id
  WHERE cac.assign_count = 1
    AND cac.sole_owner IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT regexp_replace(trim(ai.agent_id),'^#','') AS agent_id
FROM agent_involvements ai
JOIN (
  SELECT agent_id, avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM duration)) AS avg_handle_seconds
  FROM single_assign_case_times
  GROUP BY agent_id
) avg_times ON avg_times.agent_id = ai.agent_id
WHERE ai.cases_involved > 1
ORDER BY avg_times.avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

You are working with six databases to solve this query.

     - Opportunity: Sales deals

     - OpportunityLineItem: Deal line items
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
     - Quote: Price quotes
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
     - issue__c: Custom issues
      - Fields: id, name, description__c
     - casehistory__c: Case history

     - emailmessage: Email communications

4. products_orders
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains product catalog and order data.
   - This database consists of seven tables:
     - ProductCategory: Product categories
      - Fields: Id, Name, CatalogId
     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo

     - Order: Customer orders

     - OrderItem: Order details

5. activities

{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
assign_counts AS (
  SELECT case_id, count(*) AS cnt, min(owner_id) AS owner_id
  FROM owner_assigns
  GROUP BY case_id
),
single_assign_cases AS (
  SELECT case_id, owner_id FROM assign_counts WHERE cnt = 1
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
)
SELECT sa.owner_id AS agent_id,
       count(c.id) AS cases_handled,
       avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (c.closeddate - c.createddate))) AS avg_handle_seconds
FROM single_assign_cases sa
JOIN cases_in_period c ON regexp_replace(trim(c.id),'^#','') = sa.case_id
GROUP BY sa.owner_id
HAVING count(c.id) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-09-02

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with six databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these six databases:

1. core_crm
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains core CRM data including users, accounts, and contacts from Salesforce CRMArena.
   - This database consists of three tables:
    - User: Sales team information
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Username, Alias, LanguageLocaleKey, EmailEncodingKey, TimeZoneSidKey, LocaleSidKey
    - Account: Company/customer data
      - Fields: Id, Name, Phone, Industry, Description, NumberOfEmployees, ShippingState
    - Contact: Individual contacts
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, AccountId

2. sales_pipeline
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains sales pipeline data including opportunities, quotes, contracts, and leads.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Contract: Signed contracts
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, StartDate, CustomerSignedDate, CompanySignedDate, Description, ContractTerm
     - Lead: Sales leads
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, Status, ConvertedContactId, ConvertedAccountId, Title, CreatedDate, ConvertedDate, IsConverted, OwnerId
     - Opportunity: Sales deals
      - Fields: Id, ContractID__c, AccountId, ContactId, OwnerId, Probability, Amount, StageName, Name, Description, CreatedDate, CloseDate
     - OpportunityLineItem: Deal line items
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
     - Quote: Price quotes
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
     - issue__c: Custom issues
      - Fields: id, name, description__c
     - casehistory__c: Case history
      - Fields: id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
     - emailmessage: Email communications
      - Fields: id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
     - livechattranscript: Chat logs
      - Fields: id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid

4. products_orders
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains product catalog and order data.
   - This database consists of seven tables:
     - ProductCategory: Product categories
      - Fields: Id, Name, CatalogId
     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
     - PricebookEntry: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Pricebook2Id, Product2Id, UnitPrice
     - Order: Customer orders
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, EffectiveDate, Pricebook2Id, OwnerId
     - OrderItem: Order details
      - Fields: Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId

5. activities
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains activity data including tasks, events, and voice call transcripts.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - Event: Calendar events
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, StartDateTime, Subject, Description, DurationInMinutes, Location, IsAllDayEvent
     - Task: Activities and tasks
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, Priority, Status, ActivityDate, Subject, Description
     - VoiceCallTranscript__c: Call records
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId__c, LeadId__c, Body__c, CreatedDate, EndTime__c

6. territory
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains sales territory management data.
   - This database consists of two tables:
     - Territory2: Sales territories
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description
     - UserTerritory2Association: Territory assignments
      - Id, UserId, Territory2Id

{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
assign_counts AS (
  SELECT case_id, count(*) AS cnt, min(owner_id) AS owner_id
  FROM owner_assigns
  GROUP BY case_id
),
single_assign_cases AS (
  SELECT case_id, owner_id FROM assign_counts WHERE cnt = 1
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
)
SELECT sa.owner_id AS agent_id,
       count(c.id) AS cases_handled,
       avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (c.closeddate - c.createddate))) AS avg_handle_seconds
FROM single_assign_cases sa
JOIN cases_in_period c ON regexp_replace(trim(c.id),'^#','') = sa.case_id
GROUP BY sa.owner_id
HAVING count(c.id) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
),
case_assign_counts AS (
  SELECT ca.case_id, count(*) AS assign_count,
         min(ca.owner_id) FILTER (WHERE ca.owner_id IS NOT NULL) AS sole_owner
  FROM owner_assigns ca
  WHERE ca.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY ca.case_id
),
agent_involvements AS (
  SELECT oa.owner_id AS agent_id, count(DISTINCT oa.case_id) AS cases_involved
  FROM owner_assigns oa
  WHERE oa.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY oa.owner_id
),
single_assign_case_times AS (
  SELECT cac.sole_owner AS agent_id,
         (c.closeddate - c.createddate) AS duration
  FROM case_assign_counts cac
  JOIN cases_in_period c ON c.id IS NOT NULL AND regexp_replace(trim(c.id),'^#','') = cac.case_id
  WHERE cac.assign_count = 1
    AND cac.sole_owner IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT regexp_replace(trim(ai.agent_id),'^#','') AS agent_id
FROM agent_involvements ai
JOIN (
  SELECT agent_id, avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM duration)) AS avg_handle_seconds
  FROM single_assign_case_times
  GROUP BY agent_id
) avg_times ON avg_times.agent_id = ai.agent_id
WHERE ai.cases_involved > 1
ORDER BY avg_times.avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-09-02

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with six databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these six databases:

1. core_crm
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains core CRM data including users, accounts, and contacts from Salesforce CRMArena.
   - This database consists of three tables:
    - User: Sales team information
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Username, Alias, LanguageLocaleKey, EmailEncodingKey, TimeZoneSidKey, LocaleSidKey
    - Account: Company/customer data
      - Fields: Id, Name, Phone, Industry, Description, NumberOfEmployees, ShippingState
    - Contact: Individual contacts
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, AccountId

2. sales_pipeline
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains sales pipeline data including opportunities, quotes, contracts, and leads.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Contract: Signed contracts
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, StartDate, CustomerSignedDate, CompanySignedDate, Description, ContractTerm
     - Lead: Sales leads
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, Status, ConvertedContactId, ConvertedAccountId, Title, CreatedDate, ConvertedDate, IsConverted, OwnerId
     - Opportunity: Sales deals
      - Fields: Id, ContractID__c, AccountId, ContactId, OwnerId, Probability, Amount, StageName, Name, Description, CreatedDate, CloseDate
     - OpportunityLineItem: Deal line items
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
     - Quote: Price quotes
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
     - issue__c: Custom issues
      - Fields: id, name, description__c
     - casehistory__c: Case history
      - Fields: id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
     - emailmessage: Email communications
      - Fields: id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
     - livechattranscript: Chat logs
      - Fields: id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid

4. products_orders
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains product catalog and order data.
   - This database consists of seven tables:
     - ProductCategory: Product categories
      - Fields: Id, Name, CatalogId
     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
     - PricebookEntry: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Pricebook2Id, Product2Id, UnitPrice
     - Order: Customer orders
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, EffectiveDate, Pricebook2Id, OwnerId
     - OrderItem: Order details
      - Fields: Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId

5. activities
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains activity data including tasks, events, and voice call transcripts.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - Event: Calendar events
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, StartDateTime, Subject, Description, DurationInMinutes, Location, IsAllDayEvent
     - Task: Activities and tasks
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, Priority, Status, ActivityDate, Subject, Description
     - VoiceCallTranscript__c: Call records
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId__c, LeadId__c, Body__c, CreatedDate, EndTime__c

6. territory
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains sales territory management data.
   - This database consists of two tables:
     - Territory2: Sales territories
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description
     - UserTerritory2Association: Territory assignments
      - Id, UserId, Territory2Id

{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
assign_counts AS (
  SELECT case_id, count(*) AS cnt, min(owner_id) AS owner_id
  FROM owner_assigns
  GROUP BY case_id
),
single_assign_cases AS (
  SELECT case_id, owner_id FROM assign_counts WHERE cnt = 1
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
)
SELECT sa.owner_id AS agent_id,
       count(c.id) AS cases_handled,
       avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (c.closeddate - c.createddate))) AS avg_handle_seconds
FROM single_assign_cases sa
JOIN cases_in_period c ON regexp_replace(trim(c.id),'^#','') = sa.case_id
GROUP BY sa.owner_id
HAVING count(c.id) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
),
case_assign_counts AS (
  SELECT ca.case_id, count(*) AS assign_count,
         min(ca.owner_id) FILTER (WHERE ca.owner_id IS NOT NULL) AS sole_owner
  FROM owner_assigns ca
  WHERE ca.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY ca.case_id
),
agent_involvements AS (
  SELECT oa.owner_id AS agent_id, count(DISTINCT oa.case_id) AS cases_involved
  FROM owner_assigns oa
  WHERE oa.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY oa.owner_id
),
single_assign_case_times AS (
  SELECT cac.sole_owner AS agent_id,
         (c.closeddate - c.createddate) AS duration
  FROM case_assign_counts cac
  JOIN cases_in_period c ON c.id IS NOT NULL AND regexp_replace(trim(c.id),'^#','') = cac.case_id
  WHERE cac.assign_count = 1
    AND cac.sole_owner IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT regexp_replace(trim(ai.agent_id),'^#','') AS agent_id
FROM agent_involvements ai
JOIN (
  SELECT agent_id, avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM duration)) AS avg_handle_seconds
  FROM single_assign_case_times
  GROUP BY agent_id
) avg_times ON avg_times.agent_id = ai.agent_id
WHERE ai.cases_involved > 1
ORDER BY avg_times.avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),

),

  SELECT ca.case_id, min(ca.owner_id) AS owner_id
  FROM owner_assigns ca
  WHERE ca.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY ca.case_id
  HAVING count(*) = 1
),
agent_involvements AS (
  SELECT oa.owner_id AS owner_id, count(DISTINCT oa.case_id) AS involvement_count
  FROM owner_assigns oa
  WHERE oa.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY oa.owner_id
)
SELECT regexp_replace(trim(ai.owner_id),'^#','') AS agent_id,
       avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (cip.closeddate - cip.createddate))) AS avg_handle_seconds,
       count(sac.case_id) AS num_single_assigned_cases,
       ai.involvement_count
FROM agent_involvements ai
JOIN single_assign_cases sac ON sac.owner_id = ai.owner_id
JOIN cases_in_period cip ON regexp_replace(trim(cip.id),'^#','') = sac.case_id
WHERE ai.involvement_count > 1
GROUP BY ai.owner_id, ai.involvement_count
ORDER BY avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-09-02

     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid

{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
assign_counts AS (
  SELECT case_id, count(*) AS cnt, min(owner_id) AS owner_id
  FROM owner_assigns
  GROUP BY case_id
),
single_assign_cases AS (
  SELECT case_id, owner_id FROM assign_counts WHERE cnt = 1
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
)
SELECT sa.owner_id AS agent_id,
       count(c.id) AS cases_handled,
       avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (c.closeddate - c.createddate))) AS avg_handle_seconds
FROM single_assign_cases sa
JOIN cases_in_period c ON regexp_replace(trim(c.id),'^#','') = sa.case_id
GROUP BY sa.owner_id
HAVING count(c.id) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
),
case_assign_counts AS (
  SELECT ca.case_id, count(*) AS assign_count,
         min(ca.owner_id) FILTER (WHERE ca.owner_id IS NOT NULL) AS sole_owner
  FROM owner_assigns ca
  WHERE ca.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY ca.case_id
),
agent_involvements AS (
  SELECT oa.owner_id AS agent_id, count(DISTINCT oa.case_id) AS cases_involved
  FROM owner_assigns oa
  WHERE oa.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY oa.owner_id
),
single_assign_case_times AS (
  SELECT cac.sole_owner AS agent_id,
         (c.closeddate - c.createddate) AS duration
  FROM case_assign_counts cac
  JOIN cases_in_period c ON c.id IS NOT NULL AND regexp_replace(trim(c.id),'^#','') = cac.case_id
  WHERE cac.assign_count = 1
    AND cac.sole_owner IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT regexp_replace(trim(ai.agent_id),'^#','') AS agent_id
FROM agent_involvements ai
JOIN (
  SELECT agent_id, avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM duration)) AS avg_handle_seconds
  FROM single_assign_case_times
  GROUP BY agent_id
) avg_times ON avg_times.agent_id = ai.agent_id
WHERE ai.cases_involved > 1
ORDER BY avg_times.avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
assign_counts AS (
  SELECT case_id, count(*) AS cnt, min(owner_id) AS owner_id
  FROM owner_assigns
  GROUP BY case_id
),
single_assign_cases AS (
  SELECT case_id, owner_id FROM assign_counts WHERE cnt = 1
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
)
SELECT sa.owner_id AS agent_id,
       count(c.id) AS cases_handled,
       avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (c.closeddate - c.createddate))) AS avg_handle_seconds
FROM single_assign_cases sa
JOIN cases_in_period c ON regexp_replace(trim(c.id),'^#','') = sa.case_id
GROUP BY sa.owner_id
HAVING count(c.id) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
),
case_assign_counts AS (
  SELECT ca.case_id, count(*) AS assign_count,
         min(ca.owner_id) FILTER (WHERE ca.owner_id IS NOT NULL) AS sole_owner
  FROM owner_assigns ca
  WHERE ca.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY ca.case_id
),
agent_involvements AS (
  SELECT oa.owner_id AS agent_id, count(DISTINCT oa.case_id) AS cases_involved
  FROM owner_assigns oa
  WHERE oa.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY oa.owner_id
),
single_assign_case_times AS (
  SELECT cac.sole_owner AS agent_id,
         (c.closeddate - c.createddate) AS duration
  FROM case_assign_counts cac
  JOIN cases_in_period c ON c.id IS NOT NULL AND regexp_replace(trim(c.id),'^#','') = cac.case_id
  WHERE cac.assign_count = 1
    AND cac.sole_owner IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT regexp_replace(trim(ai.agent_id),'^#','') AS agent_id
FROM agent_involvements ai
JOIN (
  SELECT agent_id, avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM duration)) AS avg_handle_seconds
  FROM single_assign_case_times
  GROUP BY agent_id
) avg_times ON avg_times.agent_id = ai.agent_id
WHERE ai.cases_involved > 1
ORDER BY avg_times.avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
),
single_assign_cases AS (
  SELECT ca.case_id, min(ca.owner_id) AS owner_id
  FROM owner_assigns ca
  WHERE ca.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY ca.case_id
  HAVING count(*) = 1
),
agent_involvements AS (
  SELECT oa.owner_id AS owner_id, count(DISTINCT oa.case_id) AS involvement_count
  FROM owner_assigns oa
  WHERE oa.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY oa.owner_id
)
SELECT regexp_replace(trim(ai.owner_id),'^#','') AS agent_id,
       avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (cip.closeddate - cip.createddate))) AS avg_handle_seconds,
       count(sac.case_id) AS num_single_assigned_cases,
       ai.involvement_count
FROM agent_involvements ai
JOIN single_assign_cases sac ON sac.owner_id = ai.owner_id
JOIN cases_in_period cip ON regexp_replace(trim(cip.id),'^#','') = sac.case_id
WHERE ai.involvement_count > 1
GROUP BY ai.owner_id, ai.involvement_count
ORDER BY avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

function.arguments:
{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
assign_counts AS (
  SELECT case_id, count(*) AS cnt, min(owner_id) AS owner_id
  FROM owner_assigns
  GROUP BY case_id
),
single_assign_cases AS (
  SELECT case_id, owner_id FROM assign_counts WHERE cnt = 1
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
)
SELECT sa.owner_id AS agent_id,
       count(c.id) AS cases_handled,
       avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (c.closeddate - c.createddate))) AS avg_handle_seconds
FROM single_assign_cases sa
JOIN cases_in_period c ON regexp_replace(trim(c.id),'^#','') = sa.case_id
GROUP BY sa.owner_id
HAVING count(c.id) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
),
case_assign_counts AS (
  SELECT ca.case_id, count(*) AS assign_count,
         min(ca.owner_id) FILTER (WHERE ca.owner_id IS NOT NULL) AS sole_owner
  FROM owner_assigns ca
  WHERE ca.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY ca.case_id
),
agent_involvements AS (
  SELECT oa.owner_id AS agent_id, count(DISTINCT oa.case_id) AS cases_involved
  FROM owner_assigns oa
  WHERE oa.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY oa.owner_id
),
single_assign_case_times AS (
  SELECT cac.sole_owner AS agent_id,
         (c.closeddate - c.createddate) AS duration
  FROM case_assign_counts cac
  JOIN cases_in_period c ON c.id IS NOT NULL AND regexp_replace(trim(c.id),'^#','') = cac.case_id
  WHERE cac.assign_count = 1
    AND cac.sole_owner IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT regexp_replace(trim(ai.agent_id),'^#','') AS agent_id
FROM agent_involvements ai
JOIN (
  SELECT agent_id, avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM duration)) AS avg_handle_seconds
  FROM single_assign_case_times
  GROUP BY agent_id
) avg_times ON avg_times.agent_id = ai.agent_id
WHERE ai.cases_involved > 1
ORDER BY avg_times.avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-09-02

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with six databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these six databases:

1. core_crm
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains core CRM data including users, accounts, and contacts from Salesforce CRMArena.
   - This database consists of three tables:
    - User: Sales team information
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Username, Alias, LanguageLocaleKey, EmailEncodingKey, TimeZoneSidKey, LocaleSidKey
    - Account: Company/customer data
      - Fields: Id, Name, Phone, Industry, Description, NumberOfEmployees, ShippingState
    - Contact: Individual contacts
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, AccountId

2. sales_pipeline
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains sales pipeline data including opportunities, quotes, contracts, and leads.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Contract: Signed contracts
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, StartDate, CustomerSignedDate, CompanySignedDate, Description, ContractTerm
     - Lead: Sales leads
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, Status, ConvertedContactId, ConvertedAccountId, Title, CreatedDate, ConvertedDate, IsConverted, OwnerId
     - Opportunity: Sales deals
      - Fields: Id, ContractID__c, AccountId, ContactId, OwnerId, Probability, Amount, StageName, Name, Description, CreatedDate, CloseDate
     - OpportunityLineItem: Deal line items
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
     - Quote: Price quotes
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
     - issue__c: Custom issues
      - Fields: id, name, description__c
     - casehistory__c: Case history
      - Fields: id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
     - emailmessage: Email communications
      - Fields: id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
     - livechattranscript: Chat logs
      - Fields: id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid

4. products_orders
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains product catalog and order data.
   - This database consists of seven tables:
     - ProductCategory: Product categories
      - Fields: Id, Name, CatalogId
     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
     - PricebookEntry: one of the pricing and mapping tables

     - Order: Customer orders
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, EffectiveDate, Pricebook2Id, OwnerId
     - OrderItem: Order details
      - Fields: Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId

5. activities
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains activity data including tasks, events, and voice call transcripts.

function.arguments:
{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
assign_counts AS (
  SELECT case_id, count(*) AS cnt, min(owner_id) AS owner_id
  FROM owner_assigns
  GROUP BY case_id
),
single_assign_cases AS (
  SELECT case_id, owner_id FROM assign_counts WHERE cnt = 1
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
)
SELECT sa.owner_id AS agent_id,
       count(c.id) AS cases_handled,
       avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (c.closeddate - c.createddate))) AS avg_handle_seconds
FROM single_assign_cases sa
JOIN cases_in_period c ON regexp_replace(trim(c.id),'^#','') = sa.case_id
GROUP BY sa.owner_id
HAVING count(c.id) > 1
ORDER BY avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

function.arguments:
{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
),
case_assign_counts AS (
  SELECT ca.case_id, count(*) AS assign_count,
         min(ca.owner_id) FILTER (WHERE ca.owner_id IS NOT NULL) AS sole_owner
  FROM owner_assigns ca
  WHERE ca.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY ca.case_id
),
agent_involvements AS (
  SELECT oa.owner_id AS agent_id, count(DISTINCT oa.case_id) AS cases_involved
  FROM owner_assigns oa
  WHERE oa.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY oa.owner_id
),
single_assign_case_times AS (
  SELECT cac.sole_owner AS agent_id,
         (c.closeddate - c.createddate) AS duration
  FROM case_assign_counts cac
  JOIN cases_in_period c ON c.id IS NOT NULL AND regexp_replace(trim(c.id),'^#','') = cac.case_id
  WHERE cac.assign_count = 1
    AND cac.sole_owner IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT regexp_replace(trim(ai.agent_id),'^#','') AS agent_id
FROM agent_involvements ai
JOIN (
  SELECT agent_id, avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM duration)) AS avg_handle_seconds
  FROM single_assign_case_times
  GROUP BY agent_id
) avg_times ON avg_times.agent_id = ai.agent_id
WHERE ai.cases_involved > 1
ORDER BY avg_times.avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}

{"db_name":"support","query":"WITH owner_assigns AS (
  SELECT regexp_replace(trim(newvalue__c),'^#','') AS owner_id,
         regexp_replace(trim(caseid__c),'^#','') AS case_id
  FROM casehistory__c
  WHERE field__c = 'Owner Assignment'
),
cases_in_period AS (
  SELECT id, createddate::timestamp AS createddate, closeddate::timestamp AS closeddate
  FROM "Case"
  WHERE closeddate IS NOT NULL
    AND closeddate::timestamp >= '2023-05-02'::timestamp
    AND closeddate::timestamp < '2023-09-03'::timestamp
),
single_assign_cases AS (
  SELECT ca.case_id, min(ca.owner_id) AS owner_id
  FROM owner_assigns ca
  WHERE ca.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY ca.case_id
  HAVING count(*) = 1
),
agent_involvements AS (
  SELECT oa.owner_id AS owner_id, count(DISTINCT oa.case_id) AS involvement_count
  FROM owner_assigns oa
  WHERE oa.case_id IN (SELECT regexp_replace(trim(id),'^#','') FROM cases_in_period)
  GROUP BY oa.owner_id
)
SELECT regexp_replace(trim(ai.owner_id),'^#','') AS agent_id,
       avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (cip.closeddate - cip.createddate))) AS avg_handle_seconds,
       count(sac.case_id) AS num_single_assigned_cases,
       ai.involvement_count
FROM agent_involvements ai
JOIN single_assign_cases sac ON sac.owner_id = ai.owner_id
JOIN cases_in_period cip ON regexp_replace(trim(cip.id),'^#','') = sac.case_id
WHERE ai.involvement_count > 1
GROUP BY ai.owner_id, ai.involvement_count
ORDER BY avg_handle_seconds ASC
LIMIT 1;"}
